title: Energizing Venezuelan Quinoa Tagine
description: A yeasty, fluffy post-workout dish that blends quinoa with fresh green pepper in a fragrant tagine, crafted to be Ayurvedic sattvic-friendly.
instructions: Heat the tagine or heavy-bottom pot over medium heat and pour in the olive oil.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the green pepper, cumin, smoked paprika, turmeric, and harissa paste, cooking for another 2 minutes to let the spices bloom.
Pour in the cooked quinoa and chickpeas, mixing thoroughly to coat them with the aromatics.
Add the lemon juice and season with salt and pepper to taste, stirring well.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let the tagine steam for 10 minutes so the flavors meld.
Remove from heat and sprinkle the ground coriander over the top, allowing it finish cooking for 1 minute.
Check seasoning; adjust salt or pepper if needed.
Serve immediately with a side of fresh leafy greens or a squeeze of lime for extra acidity.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
